name: Debt.org
description_short: Debt.org is a non-profit debt help organization providing free educational content, financial calculators, and referrals to vetted debt relief providers across multiple debt categories.
description_long:
Debt.org operates as America's self-described Debt Help Organization, positioning itself as a comprehensive educational resource for consumers navigating personal finance challenges. The organization provides free online information, articles, calculators, and guides covering credit cards, student loans, mortgages, personal loans, taxes, bankruptcy, and general debt management strategies. Founded to serve the public with accessible financial information, Debt.org functions primarily as an educational platform and referral service rather than a direct service provider.

The company offers extensive free content including how-to guides, financial calculators, educational articles, infographics, and statistics across multiple debt categories. They provide information on debt relief options such as debt consolidation, debt settlement, student loan consolidation, and tax debt relief. The site includes dedicated sections for bankruptcy education, mortgage refinancing guidance, student loan repayment strategies, and credit card management. Users can also access tools for budgeting, bill payment planning, and financial literacy.

Death.org distinguishes itself through its breadth of free educational content without requiring upfront fees for information access. They claim to maintain "relationships with a variety of trustworthy debt service providers," suggesting a curation process for referral partners. The site covers niche topics like credit help for veterans, homebuyer education courses, and understanding different loan types. Their multi-topic approach allows consumers to research various debt solutions in one location.

The platform's primary limitation is that it functions as an informational intermediary rather than providing direct debt counseling or relief services. While they reference "trusted debt specialists," the extent of vetting and the financial incentives driving referrals are not transparently disclosed on the website. The business model—connecting users to for-profit debt service providers—creates potential conflicts of interest in recommendations, though this is common in the debt education space.
pros: Completely free access to comprehensive debt education and financial calcularies across six major debt categories
Covers specialized topics including bankruptcy filing guidance, tax debt relief, and credit help for veterans
Provides homebuyer education courses and mortgage rate tools at no cost
Offers free pre-bankruptcy counseling information and Chapter 7/13 explanations
Student loan section includes forgiveness programs, repayment options, and refinancing guidance
Interactive tools for budgeting, loan calculators, and personal finance planning
cons: Operates as a referral network to debt service providers rather than providing direct counseling, which may limit independent advice
Financial incentives from referral partners are not transparently disclosed on the site
No indication of nonprofit certification, accreditation by NFCC, or HUD approval on the website
Vetting standards and criteria for 'trustworthy' partner providers are not publicly explained
Does not appear to offer direct one-on-one credit counseling or debt management services
best_for: Consumers seeking free, general financial education before committing to paid debt services
Students researching student loan consolidation, forgiveness programs, and repayment strategies
Homeowners exploring mortgage refinancing, HELOC, and reverse mortgage options
Individuals considering bankruptcy who want to understand Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 implications
diagnosis: Debt.org is best for consumers seeking free, unbiased educational information on debt management, student loans, bankruptcy, and mortgages before pursuing paid services. The main caveat is that while information is free, the platform monetizes through referrals to debt service providers, which may influence which solutions are prominently featured, and it does not provide direct nonprofit credit counseling certified by NFCC or HUD.
services: Free debt consolidation education and options comparison
Student loan consolidation and forgiveness program information
Debt settlement and debt management guidance
Bankruptcy filing guides and pre-bankruptcy counseling resources
Credit card debt counseling and interest rate negotiation advice
Mortgage refinancing tools and homebuyer education courses
Tax debt relief information and IRS audit guidance
Personal loan and bad credit loan education
Financial calculators for budgeting and loan comparison
Articles, blogs, and infographics on personal finance topics
Referrals to debt relief service providers
Loan contract and financial agreement education
